steps.title: Convert the annual percentage decrease to a multiplier
steps.content:
Decreasing by 3.5% each year means we keep 100% − 3.5% = 96.5% of the previous year's count.

As a decimal, the multiplier is $0.965$.

steps.title: Set up the exponential decay formula
steps.content:
Starting with $6.22$ pheasants per mile in 2005, after 10 years of multiplying by $0.965$ each year:
$$6.22(0.965)^{10}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
